description: Relying on an implicit, unenforced schema agreement so a producer's change silently breaks downstream consumers.
body: Failure mode: consumers depend on a producer's current schema and semantics with no codified or enforced contract, so when the producer renames a column, changes units, or relaxes nullability, consumers break or silently misread. Detection signals: downstream failures or wrong values right after an unrelated producer deploy; no versioned contract exists; the producer was unaware of consumers. Do not treat the current shape as an implicit contract. Safer fallback: define and version a contract, validate producer output at publish, and require sign-off plus a migration for breaking changes.
